phrasal_verbs.meaning: give free of charge; reveal sth you are trying to hide
find sth or meet sb by chance
use as a source of money
get sth, especially sth that is hard to get
write all the necessary information on a cheque, etc
provide sth good, so that sth bad seems less important
save an amount of money for the future
save money little by little (for a specific purpose)

debt: owe sb a debt of gratitude
increase: an increase in sth (of a certain amount)

questions.sentence: She made a **wealth / fortune** when her tech start-up was bought by a larger company.
The new electric scooter is incredibly **economic / economical** to run compared to a petrol motorbike.
The online store will only process a return if you provide the original **receipt / bill**.
Scan your loyalty card at the **checkout / discount** and you will earn double reward points today.
I decided to **refund / exchange** the blue fitness tracker for a red one instead.
The passport turned out to be **plastic / fake**, so the traveller was detained at the border.
This limited-time **offer / bill** gives you three months of free access to the streaming platform!
Excuse me, do you have **make / change** for a twenty? I need coins for the vending machine.
I checked the booking app after dinner and the flight **price / cash** had jumped by eighty euros.

across.clue: Taste testers consistently rated this coffee .... as the smoothest on the market.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)

questions.sentence: Don't worry — I'll have the presentation ready by Thursday. You can ...... on it.
Could you please ...... out the invoice to Westfield College?

D.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: When I started university, I had to do (1) ...... a lot of the comforts I had enjoyed at home.
I lived (2) ...... a small student bursary, which barely covered my rent, but I managed to get (3) .......
Whenever I needed new textbooks or equipment, I would (4) ...... up carefully over several weeks, skipping takeaways.
Then one evening, while searching an old bag for a charger, I came (5) ...... a letter I had completely forgotten about!
Inside was a notification that I had come (6) ...... a small inheritance left to me by a distant relative!

E.title: Key word transformation (Phrases and collocations)
E.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
It's a good idea not to spend all your wages now so that you'll have some available later.
**for** Why not think about ...... later?
questions.answer: saving some money for/putting some money by for

questions.sentence:
Did your sister earn any money when she sold her old camera equipment?
**profit** Did your sister ...... when she sold her old camera equipment?

Not many students choose this advanced maths module.
**demand** There is ...... this advanced maths module.
questions.answer: little demand for/not much demand for

questions.sentence:
I didn't want to owe money to my flatmate, so I refused to borrow any.
**debt** I didn't want ...... my flatmate, so I refused to borrow any money.
questions.answer: to be in debt to/to be into debt to/to get in debt to/to get into debt to

questions.sentence:
That must have been very expensive — a week-long ski trip, Marco!
**fortune** A week-long ski trip like that must ...... Marco!

Inflation has climbed by 4% over the past six months.
**increase** There has been an ...... 4% over the past six months.

You'll have no savings left if you keep buying video games.
**it** You'll have no savings left if you keep ...... video games.

Don't waste money booking a private chef for the dinner party.
**expense** Don't go ...... booking a private chef for the dinner party.

I had to pay twenty-five euros extra for taking an additional bag on my flight!
**charged** The airline ...... twenty-five euros extra for taking an additional bag!

The charity event raised a significant sum from ticket sales alone.
**amount** The charity event raised ...... from ticket sales alone.

I can't afford to travel to Japan this year.
**enough** I don't have ...... to Japan this year.

She succeeded thanks to careful planning, determination, and, finally but importantly, team support.
**least** She succeeded thanks to careful planning, determination and, ......, team support.

The security camera recorded the shoplifter stealing a bottle of perfume.
**notice** The shop assistant didn't ...... a bottle of perfume.
questions.answer: notice the shoplifter taking/notice the shoplifter take

questions.sentence:
I'll head out to pick up some groceries while you chop the vegetables.
**shopping** I'll go and ...... while you chop the vegetables.
The sports club actually charged guests **E** ...for using the hotel pool.
The elderly woman on the street corner begged every passer-by **G** ...to give her some money.
I will never forget **C** ...learning that I had the winning ticket.
My two brothers always argue **A** ...about the economy.
The letter from the lender demanded **B** ...that I pay back my loan immediately.
After paying for the kitchen repairs, we couldn't afford **F** ...to buy a new fridge.
Sofia paid the entrance fee **D** ...to the girl at the desk and left.

G.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
G.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Technology is transforming modern education. Students are spending more time (1) ...... online platforms and apps than in traditional classrooms, learning from videos they download (2) ...... educational websites.
These platforms are happy to lend access (3) ...... learners all over the world because companies profit (4) ...... it through subscription fees.
Some educators argue that digital knowledge should belong (5) ...... everyone equally, and that governments should set money aside (6) ...... funding free online learning programmes.
